사도행전 7:1.content: The high priest said, “Are these things so?”

사도행전 7:2.content: He said, “Brothers and fathers, listen. The God of glory appeared to our father Abraham when he was in Mesopotamia, before he lived in Haran,

사도행전 7:3.content: and said to him, ‘Get out of your land and away from your relatives, and come into a land which I will show you.’

사도행전 7:4.content: Then he came out of the land of the Chaldaeans and lived in Haran. From there, when his father was dead, God moved him into this land, where you are now living.

사도행전 7:5.content: He gave him no inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on. He promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his offspring after him, when he still had no child.

사도행전 7:6.content: God spoke in this way: that his offspring would live as aliens in a strange land, and that they would be enslaved and mistreated for four hundred years.

사도행전 7:7.content: ‘I will judge the nation to which they will be in bondage,’ said God, ‘and after that they will come out, and serve me in this place.’

사도행전 7:8.content: He gave him the covenant of circumcision. So Abraham became the father of Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day. Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob became the father of the twelve patriarchs.

사도행전 7:9.content: “The patriarchs, moved with jealousy against Joseph, sold him into Egypt. God was with him,

사도행전 7:10.content: and delivered him out of all his afflictions, and gave him favor and wisdom before Pharaoh, king of Egypt. He made him governor over Egypt and all his house.

사도행전 7:11.content: Now a famine came over all the land of Egypt and Canaan, and great affliction. Our fathers found no food.

사도행전 7:12.content: But when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent out our fathers the first time.

사도행전 7:13.content: On the second time Joseph was made known to his brothers, and Joseph’s race was revealed to Pharaoh.

사도행전 7:14.content: Joseph sent and summoned Jacob, his father, and all his relatives, seventy-five souls.

사도행전 7:15.content: Jacob went down into Egypt and he died, himself and our fathers,

사도행전 7:16.content: and they were brought back to Shechem, and laid in the tomb that Abraham bought for a price in silver from the children of Hamor of Shechem.

사도행전 7:17.content: “But as the time of the promise came close which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,

사도행전 7:18.content: until there arose a different king, who didn’t know Joseph.

사도행전 7:19.content: The same took advantage of our race, and mistreated our fathers, and forced them to throw out their babies, so that they wouldn’t stay alive.

사도행전 7:20.content: At that time Moses was born, and was exceedingly handsome. He was nourished three months in his father’s house.

사도행전 7:21.content: When he was thrown out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up and reared him as her own son.

사도행전 7:22.content: Moses was instructed in all the wisdom of the Egyptians. He was mighty in his words and works.

사도행전 7:23.content: But when he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his brothers, the children of Israel.

사도행전 7:24.content: Seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him who was oppressed, striking the Egyptian.

사도행전 7:25.content: He supposed that his brothers understood that God, by his hand, was giving them deliverance; but they didn’t understand.

사도행전 7:26.content: “The day following, he appeared to them as they fought, and urged them to be at peace again, saying, ‘Sirs, you are brothers. Why do you wrong one another?’

사도행전 7:27.content: But he who did his neighbor wrong pushed him away, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge over us?

사도행전 7:28.content: Do you want to kill me, as you killed the Egyptian yesterday?’

사도행전 7:29.content: Moses fled at this saying, and became a stranger in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.

사도행전 7:30.content: “When forty years were fulfilled, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in a flame of fire in a bush.

사도행전 7:31.content: When Moses saw it, he wondered at the sight. As he came close to see, a voice of the Lord came to him,

사도행전 7:32.content: ‘I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’ Moses trembled, and dared not look.

사도행전 7:33.content: The Lord said to him, ‘Take off your sandals, for the place where you stand is holy ground.

사도행전 7:34.content: I have surely seen the affliction of my people that is in Egypt, and have heard their groaning. I have come down to deliver them. Now come, I will send you into Egypt.’

사도행전 7:35.content: “This Moses, whom they refused, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and a judge?’—God has sent him as both a ruler and a deliverer by the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.

사도행전 7:36.content: This man led them out, having worked wonders and signs in Egypt, in the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.

사도행전 7:37.content: This is that Moses, who said to the children of Israel, ‘The Lord our God will raise up a prophet for you from among your brothers, like me.’

사도행전 7:38.content: This is he who was in the assembly in the wilderness with the angel that spoke to him on Mount Sinai, and with our fathers, who received living revelations to give to us,

사도행전 7:39.content: to whom our fathers wouldn’t be obedient, but rejected him, and turned back in their hearts to Egypt,

사도행전 7:40.content: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods that will go before us, for as for this Moses, who led us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what has become of him.’

사도행전 7:41.content: They made a calf in those days, and brought a sacrifice to the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their hands.

사도행전 7:42.content: But God turned, and gave them up to serve the army of the sky, as it is written in the book of the prophets, ‘Did you offer to me slain animals and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, O house of Israel?

사도행전 7:43.content: You took up the tabernacle of Moloch, the star of your god Rephan, the figures which you made to worship. I will carry you away beyond Babylon.’

사도행전 7:44.content: “Our fathers had the tabernacle of the testimony in the wilderness, even as he who spoke to Moses commanded him to make it according to the pattern that he had seen;

사도행전 7:45.content: which also our fathers, in their turn, brought in with Joshua when they entered into the possession of the nations, whom God drove out before the face of our fathers, to the days of David,

사도행전 7:46.content: who found favor in the sight of God, and asked to find a habitation for the God of Jacob.

사도행전 7:47.content: But Solomon built him a house.

사도행전 7:48.content: However, the Most High doesn’t dwell in temples made with hands, as the prophet says,

사도행전 7:49.content: ‘heaven is my throne, and the earth a footstool for my feet. What kind of house will you build me?’ says the Lord. ‘Or what is the place of my rest?

사도행전 7:50.content: Didn’t my hand make all these things?’

사도행전 7:51.content: “You stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears, you always resist the Holy Spirit! As your fathers did, so you do.

사도행전 7:52.content: Which of the prophets didn’t your fathers persecute? They killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you have now become betrayers and murderers.

사도행전 7:53.content: You received the law as it was ordained by angels, and didn’t keep it!”

사도행전 7:54.content: Now when they heard these things, they were cut to the heart, and they gnashed at him with their teeth.

사도행전 7:55.content: But he, being full of the Holy Spirit, looked up steadfastly into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing on the right hand of God,

사도행전 7:56.content: and said, “Behold, I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”

사도행전 7:57.content: But they cried out with a loud voice and stopped their ears, then rushed at him with one accord.

사도행전 7:58.content: They threw him out of the city and stoned him. The witnesses placed their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.

사도행전 7:59.content: They stoned Stephen as he called out, saying, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!”

사도행전 7:60.content: He kneeled down, and cried with a loud voice, “Lord, don’t hold this sin against them!” When he had said this, he fell asleep.
